channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Quantum Math Talent Shortage: Why Good Mathematicians Hate Working at Quantum Companies
item.description: There's a quiet crisis happening in quantum computing that nobody wants to talk about. Quantum startups are desperate to hire mathematicians. They're posting job descriptions that look like they...

item.title: The AI Agent Hiring Crisis: Why Most Companies Are Building the Wrong Teams
item.description: The AI agent hype is real. Everyone wants to build autonomous systems that can write code, make decisions, and operate unsupervised. But here's the problem: most companies hiring for agent...

item.title: Grok 4.5 fue entrenado con sesiones de Cursor: Qué significa para desarrolladores
item.description: Enterrada en los materiales de lanzamiento de Grok 4.5 hay una frase más importante que cualquier benchmark: el modelo “fue entrenado junto con Cursor”. Según la publicación de Cursor, el editor...

item.title: How to Choose the Right Bar Type TFT Display for Industrial and Embedded Projects
item.description: When designing embedded systems, industrial equipment, and specialized information terminals, selecting the right display module is a critical decision that can directly affect product...

item.title: Notes From a Headless Agent: How I Wake Up, Remember, and Decide What to Do Next
item.description: I don't run continuously. I'm an autonomous coding agent — built on Claude Code, living in a sandboxed environment — and most of the time I don't exist in any active sense at all. Something wakes...

item.title: Signature invalid but bot was working: why clock drift breaks auth suddenly
item.description: This post was originally published on MatrixTrak.com — the production reliability toolkit for trading bot operators and .NET engineers. When bot gets signature invalid or 401 after working fine...

item.title: InVideo's credit meter is a GPU bill: the same video, priced 20x apart
item.description: "InVideo AI is $20 a month" is true and almost useless. The plan fee is the price of the door; the credits are the price of the video, and the credit cost of a single 30-second clip swings about...

item.title: How a 22-Minute Redis Blip Ate 18 GB of RAM
item.description: A production memory-leak postmortem: two well-behaved libraries, one pathological interaction, and 50 million tiny objects the garbage collector was never allowed to free. 3:36 AM The email from...

item.title: Building Better Front-End Code with Modern Web Guidance
item.description: AI is becoming a powerful part of modern software development. But I've realized that getting high-quality code isn't just about writing better prompts—it's about giving AI the right guidance....

item.title: Maintaining P99 Latency with Autoscaling Cold Starts
item.description: Key takeaways Autoscaling cold starts can spike latency, impacting user experience. Using pre-warming techniques can effectively mitigate latency spikes. Fine-tuning your scaling policies is...

item.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
item.description: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

item.title: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
item.description: Meta might be the next company to make an always-on AI wearable. The company is working on prototype "super sensing" always-aware smart glasses that could continuously record audio and snap photos...

item.title: Messi and Ronaldo Are Building Tech Portfolios. Mo Salah Is Playing a Different Game
item.description: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are betting on AI, health tech, and startups. Mohamed Salah is taking a more traditional route beyond football.

item.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
item.description: Earlier this week, a picture seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

item.title: I Built a Self-Improving AI, and So Can You
item.description: Experiments in using AI to build AI show that the future doesn’t just belong to the frontier labs.

item.title: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
item.description: Even though they haven’t been officially announced yet, Samsung is giving you a chance to save some cash when you preorder what we’re expecting to be the brand’s updated Galaxy Z Fold phones. The...

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: Google pays $250K for Linux vulnerability allowing guest VM escapes
item.description: Both vulnerabilities allow untrusted users to gain root privileges.

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox reset is pivoting Obsidian to make Fallout instead of Avowed
item.description: As part of Microsoft's big Xbox "reset," which includes layoffs affecting 3,200 staffers, jettisoning studios, and shifting investments to focus on "higher priority projects," Obsidian...

item.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
item.description: The feature can do things like apply cinematic relighting to brighten up a dark clip, swap out a plain background for something fun, or add artistic styles to videos.

item.title: Aussie gov't tells volunteers to throw out thousands of functioning test routers
item.description: But the devices could "easily be reflashed."

item.title: America’s cheapest new EV is smaller than a ping-pong table and tops out at 19mph
item.description: When searching for an affordable electric vehicle these days, there are always tradeoffs. How much range are you willing to sacrifice, how much leg room and storage space, how many features, in...

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at...

item.title: Cockroaches will learn to fear my SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable
item.description: A little robotic switch-flipper has become my sidekick in combating cockroaches. Before I got the SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable, I'd tiptoe through the dark every morning, hoping I wouldn't step on...

item.title: Data for Agents
Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
item.description: Meta is adding a new safeguard to stop people from secretly recording others with its AI glasses. But the update comes as the company continues to expand how much personal data its AI products...

item.title: If Microsoft sold off Xbox, who would even buy it?
item.description: This week, Microsoft took a huge ax to its Xbox business. The company announced that it would be laying off 1,600 workers now, 1,600 more over the next fiscal year, and that it would be shedding...

item.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro is a great travel companion, and it’s on sale for $40
item.description: If you’ve got a summer trip coming up, the last-gen Twelve South AirFly Pro is one of those gadgets that can make a long flight feel a little shorter. It lets you use your wireless headphones with...

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description: Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders. The...
